title: drift (plugin)
objectives: Move heavy work off the UI isolate

sections.markdown: `drift` (formerly moor) is Flutter's typed reactive SQL persistence library over SQLite. You define tables in Dart or .drift files; codegen produces type-safe DAOs and reactive Streams.
Use `NativeDatabase.createInBackground()` to run SQLite on a **background isolate** — the UI thread never touches disk. Combined with reactive streams, this yields buttery-smooth offline-first UIs.
Skipping schema migrations. Bump `schemaVersion` and provide a `MigrationStrategy` (or Drift's `schemaSteps`) — otherwise upgrades crash on production devices.
Put complex SQL in .drift files (SQL-first) — you get full syntax + type-checked results. On Web, target drift's WASM SQLite build.

flashcards.front: Where does drift run its SQL?
flashcards.back: Best practice: a background isolate via NativeDatabase.createInBackground().
watch() returns a Stream that emits when relevant tables change.
Bump schemaVersion + MigrationStrategy (or schemaSteps generated from .drift files).

quiz.q: For a snappy UI on large queries…
quiz.explain: Background isolate keeps the UI free.

options: watch() streams reacting to table changes

challenges.text: Define Users table; watch a list.
Migration adding a column with default backfill.
Complex JOIN in a .drift file with typed API.
Move DB to background isolate; measure jank before/after.

interviewQuestions: Why run on a background isolate?
